Title: PNB nears RM400bil asset milestone amid market headwinds
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Permodalan Nasional Bhd's (PNB) assets under management (AUM) stood at RM366.5 billion for the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025 (FY25) amid trade tensions, cautious monetary easing and geopolitical volatility.
In its Integrated Report 2025, the fund said its AUM brings closer to its LEAP-6 target of RM400 billion by 2027.
The flagship Amanah Saham Bumiputera (ASB) fund declared a dividend of 5.75 sen per unit, resulting in a record payout of RM15.3 billion to 11.4 million unitholders nationwide.
This performance was underpinned by a disciplined diversification strategy across equities, fixed income, private credit, and global markets, which cushioned volatility and ensured
sustainable returns.
"Our mandate goes beyond returns. It is about building resilience, uplifting lives and ensuring that Malaysians across generations benefit from sustainable prosperity," said chairman Tan Sri Arshad Raja Tun Uda in a statement.
PNB group chief executive officer and acting president Datuk Rizal Rickman Ramli said PNB's growth in AUM reflected the effectiveness of its customer-centric approach and disciplined investment strategy.
PNB continues to play a pivotal role in strengthening corporate Malaysia contributing to efforts to grow market capitalisation by RM100 billion over five years through coordinated collaboration with government-linked investment companies and government-linked companies.
The fund said the consolidation of Ekuiti Nasional Bhd under PNB represents a key structural development that will have lasting implications for the role PNB plays in corporate Malaysia including providing deeper capabilities to nurture Bumiputera enterprises across their growth journey, from pre- to post-listing.
The report also underscores PNB's progress in embedding sustainability across its portfolio and operations.
The organisation achieved net zero enterprise emissions for Scope 1 and Scope 2 in 2025, marking a milestone in its decarbonisation journey.
Beyond environmental commitments, PNB allocated RM100 million to social impact initiatives, including education, financial literacy, flood relief, and grassroots community upliftment, benefitting thousands of Malaysians.
"Sustainability is not an adjunct to our strategy, it is integral to our risk management, value creation and institutional credibility. Achieving net zero enterprise emissions in 2025 demonstrates that meaningful progress is possible with clear intent and disciplined execution," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Half of Malaysians yet to invest, PNB sees room to grow
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Half of Malaysians have yet to begin investing, presenting a significant opportunity for Permodalan Nasional Bhd (PNB) as it intensifies efforts to improve financial literacy and encourage long-term wealth creation.
PNB group chairman Raja Tan Sri Arshad Raja Tun Uda said a recent nationwide survey showed that despite the widespread adoption of Amanah Saham Nasional Bhd (ASNB) products, about 50 per cent of Malaysians have not started their investment journey.
"Although 50 per cent of Malaysians have not yet started their investing journey, ASNB remains the most widely adopted investment product across Gen Z, Millennials and Gen X.
"This highlights a clear opportunity for us, which, if executed well, can deliver far-reaching benefits not only for our unitholders, but for the financial resilience of the nation at large," he said in PNB's Integrated Report 2025.
To address the issue, PNB has stepped up financial literacy initiatives targeting Malaysians at different stages of life.
These include Kelab Pelaburan Bijak PNB for school students, Celik MadaniI for university students and Minggu Saham Amanah Malaysia (MSAM) for the wider public.
PNB has also partnered with the government to provide a free RM50 seed investment to one million students over the next five years through ASB or ASM Celik Madani accounts.
The funds will be locked in for five years to demonstrate the benefits of long-term investing and encourage students to make additional contributions.
Raja Arshad said the fund manager was increasingly leveraging data analytics to understand investor behaviour and design more targeted campaigns.
One such initiative is "Pandu Impianmu, Gen Labur", launched after PNB identified that many financing customers were withdrawing dividends to service financing costs instead of allowing their investments to compound over time.
The campaign forms part of the broader #KitaGenerasiLabur movement aimed at encouraging Malaysians to start investing early, invest consistently and adopt disciplined financial habits.
The financial literacy push comes as Malaysia continues to grapple with concerns over inadequate savings levels amid rising living costs and growing consumption-driven spending patterns.
"However, it is a challenge that can be addressed through stronger financial literacy and the inculcation of sound financial habits.
"To this end, we have continued to deepen and expand our efforts, building on longstanding initiatives while introducing targeted campaigns to drive behavioural change," Raja Arshad said.
Despite Malaysia's stronger-than-expected economic growth in 2025, Raja Arshad said Corporate Malaysia continued to face challenges in sustaining momentum.
The Malaysian economy expanded by 5.3 per cent during the year, surpassing earlier projections of between 4.0 per cent and 4.8 per cent, supported by domestic consumption and ongoing investments.
However, he noted that the performance of listed companies did not fully reflect the strength of the broader economy, with the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI posting a modest gain of 1.4 per cent amid continued difficulties in attracting sustained capital inflows.
"Corporate Malaysia struggled to sustain its momentum," he said, adding that the subdued market performance reflected underlying challenges despite favourable economic fundamentals.
Raja Arshad said PNB's transformation under its LEAP-6 strategy remained on track, with the organisation targeting RM400 billion in assets under management by 2027.
Beyond investment returns, he said PNB remained committed to strengthening financial literacy, improving corporate governance standards among investee companies and supporting community development initiatives to enhance the long-term prosperity of Malaysians.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Over 11,000 cops on duty, 39 hotspots identified
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor police have identified 39 hotspots across the state that will receive increased monitoring during the upcoming 16th Johor state election.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the location of these hotspots would not be disclosed as they were bound by police standard operating procedures (SOP).
He said additional personnel would be deployed to these areas.
"According to the SOP, we cannot disclose the hotspots, but we have identified about 39 areas so far. We will deploy more personnel to these areas that require extra attention," he told a press conference at the state police headquarters here today.
He said Johor police would mobilise a total force of 11,926 personnel, comprising 610 officers, 11,204 rank-and-file personnel and 112 civil servants.
This strength will be adjusted according to operational needs, with support from other units including the General Operations Force, Federal Reserve Unit, Police Air Wing and Marine Police.
He said Johor police would also receive manpower reinforcement from Bukit Aman and other state contingents, coordinated by the Bukit Aman Management Department.
"A total of 5,763 officers and personnel from external contingents will assist, and this number will cover polling day," he said.
He said operational duties had been divided into five main phases.
The first phase is the pre-election period, focusing on intelligence gathering, security threat assessments, monitoring political developments, identifying high-risk areas, inspecting nomination and polling centres, and coordinating with the Election Commission (EC) and related agencies.
The second phase is nomination day on June 27, focusing on security control at nomination centres, managing supporter movement and traffic control to ensure the process runs smoothly.
A total of 4,832 Johor police officers, personnel and civil servants will be deployed to ensure the nomination process runs smoothly, he said.
The third phase covers the campaign period from June 27 until 11.59pm on July 10.
During this period, police will step up monitoring of campaign activities, political talks, rallies and programmes organised by political parties, alongside traffic control, crowd management and crime prevention patrols.
A total of 3,791 policemen will be on duty during this phase.
The fourth phase covers early voting on July 7 and polling day on July 11, where policemen will be stationed at all polling centres designated by the EC.
Ab Rahaman said duties during this phase would include security at polling centres, escorting ballot boxes, crime prevention patrols, traffic management and immediate action against any incidents that could disrupt the voting process.
He said 11,814 policemen would be deployed on polling day to ensure a smooth process.
The final phase is the post-election period, where duties will continue until the counting of votes and official results announcement are completed and the security situation returns to normal.
The Johor legislative assembly was dissolved on June 1 to pave the way for the democratic process of forming a new state government mandate.
The EC, following a special meeting on June 12, announced the key dates for the Johor election, with polling day fixed for July 11.
The electoral roll for Johor police early voters, gazetted by the EC on May 21, 2026, stands at 12,710 people.
Ab Rahaman said Johor police were fully prepared for the 16th Johor state election.
"In line with our role as the main enforcement agency, we are committed to ensuring the entire election process takes place in a peaceful, orderly, safe and controlled environment to preserve public order and the well-being of the people," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Unemployed man pleads guilty to drink-driving charge in Johor
Article:
BATU PAHAT: An unemployed man pleaded guilty at the magistrate's court here today to a charge of drink-driving committed two months ago.
Khow Yong Boon, 26, entered the plea before magistrate Arun Noval Dass after the charge was read to him under Section 45A(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 (Amendment 2020).
He was accused of driving under the influence of alcohol with a blood-alcohol level of 138mg/100ml, above the legal limit of 50mg/100ml.
The offence was committed at Jalan Persiaran Flora Utama here at 3.24am on April 24.
Khow was detained during an Op Mabuk operation conducted by Batu Pahat district police traffic investigators to detect drink-driving offences.
He later tested positive for alcohol during a breathalyser test and his vehicle was impounded.
Deputy public prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan prosecuted, while Khow was represented by lawyer Suhaila Shafi'uddin.
The court fixed tomorrow for the presentation of facts and sentencing, and allowed bail of RM7,000, which was posted.
Section 45A(1) carries a maximum fine of RM30,000, up to two years' imprisonment, and disqualification from holding a driving licence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Journeys of resilience behind STPM success stories for duo
Article:
SELAYANG: An Orang Asli student and a visually-impaired candidate were among the 18 national top achievers in the Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (STPM) 2025 examination, each overcoming personal challenges to excel academically.
For Hazaril Hakimi Hassan, who is of Jah Hut and Jakun heritage, staying in his school hostel allowed him to maintain a structured study routine, supported by dedicated teachers and friends.
Hakimi, from Kampung Paya Mendoi in Kuala Krau, Temerloh, said receiving the award was a moment of gratitude after years of sacrifice by his parents, teachers and himself.
"I am grateful because after all the sacrifices made by my teachers, parents and myself, I received this award from the council," he said when met at the 2025 STPM Malaysian Examinations Council awards ceremony today.
The elder of two siblings said he aspires to become a Bahasa Melayu lecturer and plans to pursue a degree in Malay language education at Universiti Putra Malaysia.
He also recalled the daily challenges of travelling more than 30km from his village, often via difficult road conditions.
Meanwhile, visually-impaired Yeoh Chwen Yih said discipline, assistive technology and strong support from those around him were key to his academic success.
[[nid:1466503]]
He said he had a demanding daily routine, where he attended classes during the day before spending his evenings completing assignments and revising lessons.
As a blind student, said Yeoh, he relied on Braille textbooks and assistive software installed on his laptop to access learning materials.
"We use Braille textbooks and laptops equipped with software that reads digital documents aloud," he said.
He said classroom learning could be challenging sometimes, particularly when teachers wrote notes on the board.
"If the teacher writes something on the board and does not explain it clearly, I will ask my friends for help.
"Sometimes they take pictures of the notes and I use artificial intelligence tools to scan the images and convert them into text," he said.
Yeoh, who aspires to become a lawyer, said the legal profession appeals to him for its emphasis on analytical thinking.
"STPM is not an easy path, but I think it is worth it because of the quality of the qualification," he said.
Meanwhile, among the top scorers, Ng Yu Yong, Jeremy Lim Wei Yu, Chun Meng Hao, Neoh Li Boon, Tam Juin Vern, Tan Yu Jie and Ng Zhen Hong were named the seven best Science stream students, each scoring a CGPA of 4.0.
In the Social Science stream, Ahmad Mursyeid Umair Darwis, Marisya Kamila Mohamad Hafiz, Poh Qing Kang, Siti Maisarah Yahya Lotfi, Wan Nur Mahirah Wan Harun, Amelie Gan Yee Wen and Nurul Danish Farhana Mohd Fauzi were also named top scorers with a CGPA of 4.0.
Hakimi was awarded the best 2025 STPM Orang Asli student, while Nanthini Ramu was the best student in the disabled category scoring 4.0 CGPA.
Yeoh was named the best blind student, while Saw Xin En was named the best myopic student, both also achieving a CGPA of 4.0.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bird flu kills 13,000 seal pups on remote Australian island
Article:
SYDNEY: A pathogenic strain of bird flu killed more than 13,000 elephant seal pups after infecting a breeding colony on a sub-Antarctic volcanic island, Australian scientists said Thursday.
Researchers found the remote Heard and McDonald Islands littered with seal carcasses when they arrived on a research expedition in October 2025.
Genetic testing confirmed the contagious H5 bird flu strain had killed seals, penguins and birds living on the rocky outcrop – the first time it had been detected in one of Australia's external territories.
Southern elephant seal pups were the worst hit by the outbreak, scientists from the Australian Antarctic Programme said, with mortality in some seal "harems" as high as 97 per cent.
Ground and aerial surveys conducted in October 2025 and January 2026 counted 13,300 dead elephant seal pups.
"These observations of H5 bird flu at Heard Island and McDonald Island are the first detection in an Australian external territory and show the continued eastward movement of the virus," biologist Julie McInnes said.
[[nid:1466500]]
The researchers said the virus had likely been introduced in August 2025 by infected wildlife that travelled from the Crozet Islands, a sub-Antarctic archipelago about 1,500 kilometres to the northwest.
The Heard and McDonald Islands – uninhabited by humans – unexpectedly hit the headlines when they were included on US President Donald Trump's list of international tariffs in April 2025.
Lying 4,000 kilometres southwest of mainland Australia, the islands are fearsomely wild.
Access is allowed only with permission from the Australian government.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas' iron-fisted attitude increasingly evident - Tun Faisal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz said the recent changes in Perikatan Nasional's leadership structure reflect Pas' efforts to consolidate its influence within the coalition.
He said the removal of Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali and Datuk Dr Mohd Radzi Md Jidin from the coalition's supreme council has increased Pas' representation on the council to eight members.
"In contrast, Bersatu is left with only four representatives," he said in a Facebook post.
Tun Faisal described Pas' political manoeuvring as harsh.
"Day by day, little by little, Allah reveals Pas' authoritarian attitude and dirty politics. If it is not the 'Mullah' or 'Taliban' approach, then perhaps it is a Machiavellian stance, where the ends justify the means.
"It is no longer a secret that there are quarters within Pas that intend to take full control of PN and remove Bersatu from the coalition."
In terms of total votes, Tun Faisal said Gerakan and MIPP each hold three votes in the supreme council.
"Before Radzi and Azmin were removed, Pas had six representatives in the council, and Bersatu also had six.
"If voting takes place, and MIPP supports Bersatu while Gerakan supports Pas, both sides would have nine votes, making the chairman's vote the deciding factor.
"Now, even with MIPP's support, Bersatu's bloc would only have seven votes. With Gerakan's backing, Pas-Gerakan would have 10 votes (excluding the Chairman's vote), giving the party a clear advantage and confidence that decisions would favour its position."
Tun Faisal added that an emergency PN supreme council meeting had also been announced, allegedly to strengthen the coalition's organisational structure.
"The question is, why is an emergency meeting suddenly necessary when previously the PN chairman (Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar) showed little commitment to managing PN's internal conflicts, did not convene seat negotiations, election preparation meetings, or even meetings of the electoral pact committee.
"In fact, several weeks earlier, the PN chairman appeared to have 'disappeared', without making any statements on current national issues or PN's internal disputes."
Tun Faisal then said: "To my friends in Pas, InsyaAllah, Bersatu is committed to fighting for and defending PN.
"But I also hope my friends in Pas can help stop the greed and madness of this 'Publika Cluster' from within."
Samsuri, in a statement yesterday, said
Azmin was removed as deputy secretary-general, while Radzi was relieved of his duties as election director-general
.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Muda: Good ties with Bersatu in Johor, but no cooperation deal yet
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Muda has good ties with Bersatu in Johor, but no decision has been made on electoral cooperation ahead of the state election.
Its president Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz said Muda had engaged with all opposition parties ahead of the Johor state election but stressed that no conclusive decision had been reached on any electoral arrangement beyond its existing partnership with Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM).
"We do have a good relationship with Bersatu in Johor. However, there is no final decision yet.
"With Muda's decision to contest under the Progressive Bloc, the most important thing before we finalise cooperation with any other party is whether they are prepared to champion progressive policies," she told reporters after attending the launch of the Multi-Party Democracy Caucus Annual Report and Policy Paper.
Amira was responding to a question on reports quoting Bersatu deputy president Datuk Seri Ahmad Faizal Azumu as saying that Bersatu in Johor had begun discussions and was mobilising machinery alongside Muda and Pejuang.
Bersatu forges ahead as PN drags feet in Johor, Negri Sembilan polls
She said Muda had consistently kept communication channels open with opposition parties, but any cooperation would have to be guided by shared policy priorities.
"We have already begun discussions with all opposition parties. However, there is still no final decision, especially when there is still uncertainty surrounding the current status of Perikatan Nasional," she said.
She added that discussions with other opposition parties remained ongoing but urged all sides to conclude negotiations quickly so attention could be focused on issues affecting voters.
"Any decisions should be finalised as soon as possible so that we can move on to what is more important, namely the issues and policy proposals that we want to win," she said.
While declining to reveal the number of seats the party intends to contest, she said Muda had succeeded in ensuring that women make up 50 per cent of its candidates.
"We will announce the candidates in the near future. What I can confidently share is that once again, Muda has managed to field 50 per cent women candidates," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pasir Gudang 'chemical odour' traced to oil dumped in residential drain
Article:
PASIR GUDANG: Taman Tanjung Puteri Resort residents here were left worried after a mysterious chemical odour hung in their neighbourhood on Saturday.
Johor Department of Environment (DoE) director Dr Mohd Famey Yusoff said complaints were received regarding chemical odour pollution in the residential area at 10pm.
He said DoE personnel, accompanied by police, were deployed to the scene to investigate and identify the source.
"Investigations revealed a strong stench resembling that of oil and traces of black oil was found the main drain of the housing estate.
"Further inspection led to the discovery of oil discharge at a nearby residence, which is believed to be the source of the odour pollution.
"A man suspected responsible of dumping the oil into the drain has been identified and detained to assist investigations," he said in a statement today.
He said immediate cleanup operations were carried out by a contractor appointed by the Pasir Gudang City Council.
"If investigations reveal any discharge, spillage, or pollution that violates the Environmental Quality Act 1974, appropriate enforcement action will be taken against the suspect," he added.
Famey reminded all parties not to dump oil, chemical substances, or scheduled waste into drains.
He said such actions cause environmental pollution and threaten the health and well-being of local residents.
Investigations are ongoing to identify the type and source of the oil.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Political stability crucial to Malaysia's high-income goal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia can sustain its ambition of becoming a high-income nation if its economic momentum continues, but factors such as political instability could pose challenges to achieving that goal.
Deputy Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Sim Tze Tzin said maintaining gross domestic product (GDP) growth and the current economic trajectory would strengthen Malaysia's prospects of crossing the high-income threshold.
"If our GDP can maintain and if we can sustain our economic momentum, we hope Malaysia will be able to become a high-income country and cross that threshold.
"We are heading in that direction, maybe within two years, but it depends on many factors that are beyond our control," he said after attending the Malaysian International Chamber of Commerce and Industry's 53rd annual general meeting.
Sim said external factors, including global trade conditions, economic uncertainties, supply chain disruptions and rising living costs, could affect Malaysia's path towards achieving high-income status.
"These are factors that are beyond our control at this point. But to me, if we continue with the momentum we achieved in 2025, we will be able to achieve it. However, a lot of uncertainty remains," he said.
Sim said collective efforts from the government, private sector, workers and Malaysians as a whole would be essential to improving productivity and driving economic growth.
"Rule number one is that we have to work hard. The government must work hard, the private sector must work hard, workers must work hard, and all Malaysians must work hard in terms of productivity and other areas," he said.
Beyond economic factors, Sim said political stability would be equally important in supporting Malaysia's transition towards a high-income economy.
"If we have continuous political chaos, like what happened from 2020 to 2023, this instability will create problems for us in moving towards a high-income nation," he said.
Sim said Malaysians must recognise that much is at stake, noting that political stability is crucial to ensuring the country remains on track towards achieving its economic ambitions.
"If we disrupt politics, if we do not have a stable government and continue changing governments all the time, then we will be left behind," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor beats national STPM average, 136 score perfect CGPA
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor outperformed the national average in the Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (STPM) examination last year, recording a cumulative grade point average (CGPA) of 2.94 compared with the national CGPA of 2.88.
Caretaker Johor Education and Information Committee chairman Aznan Tamin congratulated the state's STPM candidates after the results were released today, describing the achievement as a testament to the dedication of students, teachers, parents and the wider education community.
A total of 3,556 candidates registered for the examination, with 3,436 candidates, or 96.63 per cent of those who sat for the papers, qualifying for the STPM certificate.
Of the total, 136 candidates achieved a perfect CGPA of 4.00, including one student who obtained 5A and 135 others who secured 4A results.
Johor also recorded an improvement in the percentage of candidates obtaining a full pass in four subjects, rising from 77.91 per cent to 80.73 per cent, Aznan said in a statement today.
In addition, Johor secured two major national accolades, namely the Best STPM 2025 Student in the Science Stream and the National Best Form Six College Award.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: India sparred with Telegram days ahead of blocking the app
Article:
INDIA privately rebuked Telegram for not proactively removing accounts offering purported leaked exam papers while the company accused New Delhi of misrepresenting meeting records, an impasse that led to an unprecedented ban of the app, documents show.
Telegram has challenged the ban by Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government in the Delhi High Court.
The government invoked emergency powers to block Telegram in the country until June 22 amid concerns the platform was being abused to defraud candidates for NEET, a prestigious national entrance test for medical colleges.
The exam results were cancelled in May on suspicion that question papers were leaked, and the test been rescheduled for June 21.
Telegram, which has 150 million users in India, its biggest market, has said in its court papers the ban undermines constitutional protections and free speech rights.
The fight marks the latest tussle between a tech company and Modi after the government fought Elon Musk's X last year in court over the company's strict restrictions on taking down content.
Documents seen by Reuters show the June 16 Telegram ban order followed two weeks of back-and-forth exchanges and meetings between India's IT ministry and company officials, with New Delhi accusing Telegram of "inaction" on channels like "NEET PAPER LEAKED" and "Paper Leaked NEET" which it said clearly indicated their suspicious nature.
Some demanded money by claiming they could provide the "full (exam) paper."
Telegram retorted in subsequent e-mails, saying it was "surprised at the suggestion that it has been inactive in addressing unlawful content" and it does not permit the use of its services for any such activity.
India's IT ministry and Telegram did not respond to Reuters queries.
WhatsApp is the most popular messaging app in India with over 500 million users, but Telegram has a unique attraction.
Its groups can hold up to 200,000 members - far beyond WhatsApp's cap of 1,024, and it allows users to interact without exposing a phone number.
Those features have also made it a favoured platform for fraud and other illicit trade, critics say, though Telegram denies these allegations and says it acts promptly against rogue elements.
The Indian government says the issue of purported frauds linked to the NEET exam is "most pronounced on Telegram."
The undergraduate medical entrance exam at the centre of the dispute is taken by more than two million students, and the cancellation of the results caused a political storm, including demands for the resignation of the country's education minister.
Telegram's founder Pavel Durov has said the Indian government's ban "is a mistake" which punishes its users while leaks can just move to other apps.
It has also taken a dig at the move without referring to India.
"Over 300,000 people die of drowning each year. In order to protect society, it is now illegal to consume or possess water," Telegram said in one post on its X account.
Behind closed doors, tensions rose between Telegram and Indian officials after a June 3 meeting, with the company saying in an e-mail to Indian officials that minutes of the meeting did not accurately capture their discussions.
The government records said Telegram stated in the meeting it had limitations in proactively detecting "more subjective" content linked to exams, instead of "objective issues" like child sexual material and pornography.
Telegram rebutted, writing in a June 5 e-mail that it was not that it didn't have proactive measures for such content, but it was only that they require more moderation.
In its court petition against the ban, the messaging app has taken stronger objection to the government's minutes, calling them a "one-sided and inaccurate account of the discussions" that "deliberately" omitted details of the company's proactive processes.
The government is yet respond to those allegations in court.
* The writers are from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Removable sunshades, curtains remain illegal on moving vehicles, says RTD
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Public confusion over the use of removable sunshades and curtains on vehicle windows has resurfaced following recent discussions on social media, with many motorists questioning why such accessories are prohibited while certain tinted windows are allowed.
Under Malaysian road transport regulations, the use of removable sunshades, curtains or other window coverings while a vehicle is in motion is an offence.
The Road Transport Department (RTD) regulates vehicle window modifications under the Motor Vehicles (Prohibition of Certain Types of Glass) Rules 1991, which set out requirements on window transparency and road safety.
Although the regulations mainly govern window tinting, enforcement authorities have also classified curtains, blinds and removable sunshades as prohibited obstructions when a vehicle is being driven.
RTD has previously explained that such obstructions may create blind spots, reduce a driver's field of vision and compromise road safety. They can also hinder enforcement officers from seeing inside a vehicle when carrying out inspections.
However, the issue continues to cause confusion among motorists because current regulations permit darker tinting on rear and rear passenger windows, provided they meet approved Visible Light Transmission (VLT) requirements.
Under existing rules, front windscreens and front side windows must meet minimum VLT standards to ensure sufficient visibility. Rear windows, however, may have darker tint levels as long as they comply with RTD specifications.
Many motorists have questioned why compliant tint films are allowed while removable sunshades and curtains are prohibited.
The renewed debate has prompted calls for RTD to provide clearer guidance on enforcement practices and the legal interpretation of regulations governing vehicle window modifications.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Macron calls for global AI rules, warns of risks
Article:
EVIAN: French President Emmanuel Macron on Wednesday called for stronger international regulation of artificial intelligence (AI), warning that its impact on democratic societies can no longer be ignored, German Press Agency (dpa) reported.
"We all want to harness the opportunities, productivity gains, changes, and improvements in health and digitalisation that AI can bring," Macron said at the close of the G7 summit in Evian.
"But no one can any longer ignore the impact of AI on our democracies and our societies," he said, arguing that regulation was needed at the international level.
Against the backdrop of a recent United States (US) government-directed move by Anthropic to restrict access to some of its most advanced AI models for foreign nationals, Macron called for closer cooperation among democratic countries.
Better regulation of advanced AI systems was needed "to prevent them from falling into the hands of authoritarian regimes or individuals who could threaten our cybersecurity or our societies," he said.
Macron said several democratic powers would begin building a cooperation platform aimed at establishing common AI standards, including in the field of cybersecurity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MMEA seizes cigarettes worth RM212k in Kelantan river operation
Article:
PASIR PUTEH: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) seized 24 boxes of contraband cigarettes worth about RM212,000 at Kampung Penambang last week.
Kelantan MMEA director Maritime Captain Kamal Mohamad said no arrests were made during the 9pm operation on June 13.
He said the seizure followed intelligence on a boat suspected of being involved in smuggling activities near the Kelantan River estuary.
"Our personnel detected a boat moving under the cover of darkness during Op Naga along the river and monitored it before it fled through a narrow waterway," he said.
MMEA personnel later found a stack of brown boxes believed to contain duty-unpaid cigarettes on the riverbank at Penambang in Kota Baru and discovered three abandoned multi-purpose vehicles loaded with suspected cigarettes nearby.
Kamal said the estimate included the vehicles seized in the operation.
The case is being investigated under Section 135(e) of the Customs Act 1967 for transporting and transferring dutiable, uncustomed or prohibited goods.
Anyone found guilty of the offence is liable to a fine, imprisonment or both.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia central bank raises rates by 25 basis points
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia's central bank raised its benchmark rates by 25 basis points on Thursday, just a week after a surprise off-cycle rate hike, as it seeks to attract fresh capital inflows and halt selling in its battered rupiah and other assets.
Bank Indonesia raised the seven-day reverse repurchase rate to 5.75 per cent, the highest level since May 2025.
Twenty of 35 economists in a Reuters survey had predicted the move; two expected a 50-basis-point increase and the other 13 expected no change.
BI also raised the overnight deposit facility rate and lending facility rates by the same amount to 4.75 per cent and 6.50 per cent.
The central bank has now hiked its policy rates by a total of 100 basis points in three moves over the past four weeks.
BI's rare off-cycle increase last week reflected mounting urgency to stabilise the rupiah and anchor investor confidence, after the currency hit a series of record lows amid global volatility, capital outflows and rising domestic policy concerns.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wildlife group claims RM53mil elephant transfer payment went to individuals, not govt coffers
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: A wildlife rights group has alleged that payment from the transfer of three elephants from Malaysia to a zoo in Japan did not go into government coffers, but instead ended up in the hands of some people.
Hak Asasi Hidupan Liar Malaysia (Hidup) lawyer Sachpreetraj Singh Sohanpal said the group intended to lodge a complaint with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) over the
relocation of three elephants
—Dara, Amoi and Kelat—to the Tennoji Zoo in Osaka.
"The complaint is on whether the relocation was transparent and if money was involved," he said outside the MACC headquarters in Putrajaya, after the group was denied entry earlier today.
Fellow Hidup lawyer Rajesh Nagarajan claimed the complaint centres on suspected corruption involving the export of the animals, which he described as national treasures, with the aim of securing their return to Malaysia.
"We believe payment has been made by Japan to Malaysia, but it did not go to the government's coffers; instead, it went into the pockets of some people.
"A whistleblower alleged the elephants were sold for US$13 million (RM53 million). It is from a whistleblower, so there is no proof yet. That is why we want MACC to investigate," he said.
Rajesh also called on the MACC to probe the Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) as well as former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, saying the public deserves full transparency.
He claimed that he was threatened with arrest by security personnel during the standoff, after officers prevented the group from entering the MACC headquarters under the Protected Areas and Protected Places Act.
However, MACC Strategic Communications Division director Hisyam Mohd Yusoff denied that the group was being blocked from lodging a complaint.
He said the MACC welcomes complaints but access is restricted due to security rules.
"A large group today wanted to enter at once, which we cannot allow. This is a restricted area, but we are not stopping anyone from lodging reports.
"We have a standard operating procedure where only two people are allowed inside—the lawyer and the complainant," he said, adding that the rules were in place for security reasons and not to bar the public.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: FMM proposes crisis adjustment mechanism under SKDS
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Federation of Malaysian Manufacturing (FMM) has proposed the establishment of a Crisis Adjustment Mechanism within the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS).
Its president, Jacob Lee Chor Kok said that under such a mechanism, temporary and targeted quota adjustments could be considered when major external disruptions significantly affect transportation and supply chain operations.
"This would provide a structured avenue for businesses and transport operators to seek temporary relief based on demonstrated operational requirements while preserving the overall objectives of subsidy rationalisation," he said in a statement today.
Additionally, he said such a mechanism could be implemented through the existing Petroleum Subsidy Approval Committee with an accelerated review process during recognised disruptions.
"This would enable genuine operational requirements arising from extraordinary circumstances to be assessed quickly while maintaining the integrity of the subsidy system," Lee said.
He said FMM has formally requested an engagement session with the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) to better understand the basis for the revised quota allocations, share industry feedback and discuss practical measures to minimise unintended impact on supply chains and the cost of living.
"FMM stands ready to work closely with KPDN and other relevant agencies to develop solutions that strengthen subsidy governance while safeguarding supply chain resilience, economic competitiveness and consumer interests," he said.
KPDN recently revised the fixed quota limits under the SKDS for the goods transportation sector to strengthen regulatory controls and curb subsidy leakages and misuse of fleet cards.
Effective June 1, 2026, each fleet card will be allocated a monthly quota of between 900 litres and 5,000 litres, depending on the limits set for 23 eligible vehicle categories under SKDS.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Halim reflects on challenging first month as MACC chief
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman has pledged to drive improvements across the agency, describing his first month at the helm as a challenging but rewarding transition.
He acknowledged that leading the anti-graft agency was markedly different from his previous role as a judge, but said public service required those entrusted with responsibility to embrace challenges rather than avoid them.
"As you know, my background before this was different from the scope of work I am handling now. For me, this is a challenge, and regardless of our position, we must shoulder it.
"We cannot run away from it. I am looking forward to the betterment of the organisation as a whole," he told reporters at the MACC headquarters here today.
He was responding to a question on the challenges of adapting to his new responsibilities after being appointed MACC chief commissioner on a two-year contract.
A former High Court judge, Halim assumed office on May 13 after His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, consented to his appointment.
He took over from Tan Sri Azam Baki, who retired on May 12 after serving the anti-corruption agency for 42 years.
Since taking office, he has emphasised strengthening integrity, improving governance and enhancing public confidence in the commission, while maintaining a firm stance against corruption.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM195mil in Bersatu accounts remain frozen after court ruling
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu bank accounts containing RM195 million will remain frozen after the High Court today dismissed the party's challenge against the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission's (MACC) freezing and seizure orders.
Judge Datuk Aliza Sulaiman ruled that Bersatu and its president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin failed to establish that the respondents' decisions were tainted by illegality, irrationality or bad faith.
On Oct 30, 2023, the court allowed Bersatu to commence judicial review proceedings to challenge the MACC's decision to freeze its two accounts.
On May 29, 2023, Datuk
Muhammad Suhaimi Yahya
, as a Bersatu public officer, filed an application for leave to initiate the judicial review.
He named the then attorney-general Tan Sri Idrus Harun and MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki as the first and second respondents, respectively, along with 20 others as the third to 22nd respondents.
Bersatu claimed that on April 19, 2023, the party's CIMB and AmBank accounts were seized without any forfeiture order issued under Section 50(1) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UNHCR card holder's BMW among 145 luxury vehicles seized by RTD
Article:
SEREMBAN: A BMW 7 Series car driven by a United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cardholder was among 145 vehicles impounded by the Road Transport Department (RTD) recently.
The vehicle, driven by a man in his 30s, was seized after checks found that its road tax had expired.
The vehicle was reportedly decorated with ribbons when it was stopped last week, as it was being prepared for use at a wedding ceremony.
Negri Sembilan RTD director Jasyindar Singh Sidhu said 870 vehicles were inspected, and 145 vehicles were impounded, including various luxury models such as BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Toyota Alphard, Lexus, and a BMW Limousine.
"Throughout the operation, seven luxury vehicles involving non-citizens, primarily from China, Pakistan, and Bangladesh, including a UNHCR cardholder, were impounded," he said in a statement today.
"This operation, which has been ongoing since early this year, focuses specifically on compliance with motor vehicle licences, insurance coverage, and invalid or expired driving licences."
He said the department also focused on vehicle modifications without RTD approval and other violations under the Road Transport Act 1987.
"I would like to remind all luxury vehicle owners and automotive enthusiasts that the value or status of a vehicle does not provide any immunity from legal action," he said.
"We will increase the frequency and intensity of enforcement operations, particularly involving luxury vehicles, including through patrols, roadblocks, and technical inspections to ensure the highest level of compliance with road laws.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: inDrive records highest monthly ride volume in May with 1.2mil trips
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: E-hailing platform inDrive Malaysia has completed more than 1.2 million rides in May 2026, its highest monthly ride volume to date, driven by growth in both drivers and passengers.
Compared with January 2026, completed rides increased 8.7 per cent, while the number of active driver-partners rose 10.2 per cent and passenger numbers grew 1.9 per cent.
On a month-on-month basis, active driver-partners expanded 5.5 per cent in May from April, while passenger numbers edged up 1.1 per cent.
inDrive Malaysia driver operations manager Azlan Anwar said the platform's growth reflects the contributions of its driver-partners, who play a key role in connecting communities across the country.
"As the cost of driving continues to rise, platforms have a role in helping drivers manage expenses, earn more sustainably and feel valued for their work.
"The Platinum Driver League is one way we recognise their contributions. By providing more meaningful support to drivers, we can also improve the passenger experience and contribute to the long-term growth of Malaysia's gig economy," he said in a media sharing session here today.
Azlan said inDrive is among the country's top three e-hailing platforms.
He said the company charges drivers a commission rate of 10.99 per cent, allowing them to retain a larger share of their earnings.
"This is below the 20 per cent ceiling rate allowed by the regulator," he said.
He added that the company conducted a limited-time one per cent commission campaign in the Klang Valley from April to May 2026 to help driver-partners reduce costs and improve earnings.
He said the initiative reinforced inDrive's commitment to building a fairer and more sustainable ride-hailing ecosystem.
Meanwhile, inDrive Malaysia country lead Govin Kumaar said the company is exploring opportunities to expand its service offerings in Malaysia, although no decision has been made on introducing additional services such as courier delivery.
He said the company remains focused on growing its e-hailing business and is not ready to announce any plans for new services.
Govin said inDrive differentiates itself through its peer-to-peer pricing model, which allows passengers and drivers to negotiate fares before a ride is confirmed.
"Passengers are shown a recommended fare after entering their destination, but both passengers and drivers can adjust the amount before agreeing on a final price.
"For example, if I offer RM20 for a ride from point A to point B and the driver wants RM25, the driver can counter with RM25. The passenger then has the option to accept or reject the price.
"That is what makes it different," he said.
Looking ahead, inDrive will focus on initiatives to reduce drivers' operational costs and launch its Comfort Class service in 2026.
The company said these efforts come as competition among ride-hailing platforms in the Asia-Pacific region increasingly centres on service quality, driver availability, safety and operational efficiency.
The company has also renewed its ride-hailing operations licence, allowing it to continue operating in Malaysia until 2028.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PKFZ snags RM566mil in new investments
Article:
PORT KLANG: The Port Klang Free Zone (PKFZ) today unveiled RM566 million worth of new investments and launched its Smart Intelligent Industrial Park initiative, marking a major milestone in its transformation into a regional trade, logistics and industrial hub.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the investments would drive economic growth, create jobs, boost exports and strengthen Malaysia's competitiveness in the global marketplace.
The projects launched comprise Wisma Golden Horse, the PKFZ Centralised Labour Quarters (CLQ), Suntek Materials Sdn Bhd's factory, Golden Horse Rubber Factory Phase 2 and PKFZ's fully integrated management system under its Smart Intelligent Industrial Park initiative.
"Today's event reflects strong investor confidence in Malaysia's industrial and logistics ecosystem, as well as PKFZ's growing role as a regional trade and investment hub.
"With a cumulative investment value of RM566 million, these initiatives will drive economic growth, create jobs, boost exports and strengthen Malaysia's global competitiveness," he said during the pre-launch ceremony.
Loke said PKFZ's transformation into a Smart Intelligent Industrial Park marked a new chapter in Malaysia's digital logistics journey.
He said PKFZ had become the country's first free zone to integrate the Free Zone Management System (FZMS), Automated Gate Operation System (AGOS), Digital Port Pass and other key operational modules under a single platform.
"The adoption of a single-window ecosystem, together with the Free Zone Administration System, AI-assisted smart CCTV and drone monitoring, reflects PKFZ's commitment towards innovation, operational excellence and its vision of becoming a future-ready regional trade and logistics hub."
Loke also highlighted PKFZ's efforts to unlock value from previously underutilised assets, revealing that 86 per cent of commercial spaces that had remained dormant for nearly two decades had now been successfully reactivated.
Among the initiatives undertaken were the conversion of two eight-storey office blocks into CLQs managed by Q Centre Management (Westport) Sdn Bhd, while an eight-storey hotel building has been leased to Golden Horse Rubber as its regional corporate headquarters and renamed Wisma Golden Horse.
The exhibition centre and covered car park have also been repurposed into a vehicle storage facility for Sime Darby Auto Bavaria Sdn Bhd, while another office building has been redeveloped into a mixed-use retail and office complex featuring clinics, cafes, restaurants and a gymnasium.
"This strategic initiative demonstrates PKFZ's ability to unlock value from underutilised assets, generating sustainable revenue while fostering positive social and economic benefits for investors and the local community."
The transformation has translated into improved financial performance, with PKFZ recording its highest-ever revenue of RM108 million in 2025.
As of May this year, throughput had increased 14.9 per cent year-on-year to 110,467 twenty-foot equivalent units (TEUs), while revenue and collections rose 16 per cent and 29 per cent respectively compared with the corresponding period in 2024.
Loke said Chinese investment in PKFZ continued to expand, with occupied space by China-linked companies surpassing 3.18 million square feet as of January this year.
China-linked businesses also contributed 88,070 TEUs in 2025, accounting for 20 per cent of PKFZ's total throughput, he added.
He said concerns raised by Chinese investors over the shortage of skilled workers had prompted efforts to strengthen workforce development initiatives.
"To address the issue, PKFZ recently signed a memorandum of understanding with Ex-Servicemen Affairs Corporation (Perhebat) to create employment opportunities for military veterans and Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) graduates."
Loke said one investor, Golden Horse, had already identified about 500 job opportunities ranging from production to engineering positions.
"The government remains committed to strengthening Malaysia's position as a leading maritime, logistics and trade hub in the region through continuous infrastructure development, investment facilitation and digital transformation."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN restructuring aimed at strengthening coalition, says Tuan Ibrahim
Article:
KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional's latest leadership restructuring was undertaken to strengthen the coalition and prepare it for future challenges.
PN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the changes, which took immediate effect, were discussed during a meeting yesterday.
He said a separate meeting involving Pas members of parliament and assemblymen was also held later in the evening to discuss preparations for the upcoming Parliament sitting.
His remarks came following PN's announcement last night that Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali and Datuk Dr Mohd Radzi Md Jidin
had been dropped
from the coalition's leadership line-up.
PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar had said Azmin had been relieved of his duties as deputy secretary-general, while Radzi was removed as election director.
The restructuring took effect immediately as part of preparations for the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections.
Samsuri had said the decision was made under Clauses 8.3 (V), (VI) and (VII) of the PN constitution.
He also announced that Kedah Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor would replace Radzi as election director.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's trade momentum seen holding through 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's trade is expected to remain resilient in the coming months, although prospects for maintaining the momentum through the rest of 2026 remain cautiously optimistic, according to the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry.
Its deputy minister Sim Tze Tzin said Malaysia had delivered a remarkable performance in the first four months of the year despite mounting geopolitical tensions and disruptions to global supply chains linked to the Strait of Hormuz.
"Malaysia has recorded its highest-ever trade figures, with April marking the strongest trade performance in the country's history.
"This shows that Malaysia is resilient. We have also achieved double-digit growth with many of our major trading partners around the world. Among them are the United States, China, Taiwan, Peru, South Korea and Japan.
"We have recorded double-digit growth with these key markets, and we believe this positive trajectory will continue for the next few months," he told reporters after the Malaysian International Chamber of Commerce and Industry's 53rd annual general meeting.
Sim attributed part of the strong performance to the global artificial intelligence supercycle, which has continued to fuel demand for Malaysia's electrical and electronics exports.
"Whether this momentum can be sustained through 2026 remains to be seen, but I am cautiously optimistic given the rapidly changing global environment.
"The May trade figures will be released soon, and based on the indications we have seen so far, the performance remains very positive," he said.
Malaysia's total trade surpassed the RM1 trillion mark within the first four months of 2026, reaching the milestone at the fastest pace on record.
Total trade increased 15.3 per cent year-on-year to RM1.127 trillion, driven by a 19 per cent rise in exports to RM609.31 billion, while imports grew 11.1 per cent to RM517.4 billion.
The Statistics Department is scheduled to release details of Malaysia's external trade statistics for May on Friday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: South Korea parliament launches probe into election ballot shortages
Article:
SEOUL: South Korea's parliament on Thursday launched a 45-day parliamentary investigation into the National Election Commission after ballot-paper shortages disrupted voting in the June 3 local elections, approving the plan at a plenary session.
The ballot paper fiasco has triggered protests, the resignation of the NEC chief and calls from President Lee Jae Myung for a thorough investigation.
The special parliamentary committee will examine the NEC and regional election commissions over what lawmakers described as infringements of citizens' voting rights and the need for election-management reform.
The investigation panel comprises lawmakers from the ruling Democratic Party, main opposition People Power Party (PPP) and from minor parties, with PPP lawmaker Yoon Sang-hyun set to chair it.
"The fact-finding investigation is not the end, but the beginning," National Assembly speaker Cho Jeong-sik said.
"The parliamentary investigation should identify the causes and lead to election management reform measures that the public can trust."
On Wednesday, NEC acting secretary-general Kang Dong-wan told protesting university student representatives the commission felt "devastated" by its inadequate preparation and would cooperate with the parliamentary inquiry, a joint police-prosecution investigation and its own audit.
An NEC official said on Wednesday ballot shortages occurred at 91 polling stations nationwide, with voting briefly suspended at 26 during the local elections.
In Seoul's Songpa district, one polling station halted voting at 4.46 pm, resumed at 5.39pm and finally closed at 10pm to allow some 175 waiting ticket holders to vote, but 12 voters who had received waiting tickets did not return, the NEC official said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Muda fields 50pct women candidates for Johor polls
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Muda has finalised its list of candidates for the upcoming Johor election, with women making up 50 per cent of those selected, party president Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz said today.
She said the party's candidate selection committee had completed the vetting process and finalised the lineup, which would be announced soon.
"We have already finalised our candidates. They have gone through interviews conducted by our candidate selection committee and we will announce them in the near future.
"What I can confidently share is that, once again, Muda has fielded 50 per cent women candidates. I am very proud of that achievement," she told reporters after attending the launch of the Multi-Party Democracy Caucus Annual Report and Policy Paper..
Amira declined to reveal the number of seats Muda plans to contest in Johor, saying the announcement would be made later.
On reports of competing interest from Pakatan Harapan component parties for the Putri Wangsa seat, she said no constituency belonged permanently to any political party.
"A seat is not the absolute property of any party. When a state assembly is dissolved, every seat belongs to the people.
"Even though I served as the assemblywoman for Putri Wangsa, we do not regard it as our absolute seat," she said.
She called on political parties to stop quarrelling over seat allocations and focus instead on issues affecting voters.
"People do not want to hear political parties fighting over seats. They want to know what issues will be brought forward.
"In Putri Wangsa alone there are many issues, including floods, infrastructure, roads and drainage. These are the matters people want addressed."
On Muda's electoral strategy, Amira said that the party would contest the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections under the "progressive bloc" together with Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM).
She said both parties had agreed on a common set of progressive policies and remained open to discussions with other opposition parties that shared similar principles.
"Muda will move forward with the progressive bloc. Our cooperation with PSM has already been finalised.
"We are also open to discussions with any party that believes in progressive policies for Johor and Negri Sembilan."
Amira said they had been engaging various opposition parties, including Bersatu and Pas, to explore possible cooperation and avoid multi-cornered contests.
However, she added that no final agreement had been reached beyond its partnership with PSM.
"We have held discussions with all opposition parties, but there are no final decisions yet. What has been finalised is our cooperation with PSM.
"With only about a week left, all parties should conclude their decisions quickly so that attention can shift to the more important matters of policies and issues affecting the people."
Amira also said Muda will hold a press conference in Putri Wangsa on June 20, where the party hopes to provide clarity on the seat and any related political arrangements ahead of the election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UN warns Israeli settlers could join blacklist for violations against children
Article:
NEW YORK: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres warned on Wednesday that Israeli settler groups could be added to a global blacklist for violations against children as he voiced alarm at a "staggering" rise in violations against Palestinian children.
The world body's annual report on Children and Armed Conflict recorded 38,558 "grave violations" globally in 2025, affecting 24,174 children, the latter figure a record since CAAC's mandate began in 1996.
The data showed 14,224 children killed or maimed, with a 34 per cent rise over 2024 in the number killed to 6,266.
It said the United Nations had verified the killing of 2,668 Palestinian children in Gaza and 57 in the West Bank.
The Gaza war began on Oct 7, 2023, when Hamas-led fighters attacked southern Israel, killing about 1,200 people, according to Israeli figures.
Israel responded with a large-scale military campaign that has since killed tens of thousands of Palestinians.
"Countries with the highest levels of violations in 2025 were the Occupied Palestinian Territory and Israel, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Nigeria, Myanmar, and Somalia," a senior UN official said in a briefing on the report.
Israel itself already features in the report's so-called list of shame annexes for alleged violations, but the latest version for the first time highlights settlers as a potential future listing.
"I am appalled by the magnitude of grave violations against children in the Occupied Palestinian Territory and Israel, notably by the widespread use of explosive weapons in populated areas," Guterres said in the report.
"I am deeply alarmed at the staggering rise in attacks carried out by Israeli settlers resulting in grave violations against Palestinian children," Guterres added.
He said Israeli settler groups should be listed if the high number of violations is repeated in 2026.
The report said 9,465 grave violations were attributed to Israeli forces and 326 to Israeli settlers.
It defines grave violations as including the killing and maiming of children, rape and other forms of sexual violence, and attacks on schools and hospitals.
Israel's UN mission did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
The report continues to blacklist Hamas' armed wing and affiliated factions for killing and maiming children and for abductions, and attributes 2,806 violations to Palestinian armed groups.
The new report comes weeks after Guterres infuriated Israel by adding it to a separate UN blacklist of countries and parties suspected of committing sexual violence in conflict zones, a move that prompted Israel's foreign ministry to say it would sever all ties with him.
Guterres said he was alarmed by the high number of children detained by Israel and reports of severe physical violence and poor conditions during detention, saying these "may constitute inhuman or degrading treatment or punishment."
Being blacklisted does not trigger automatic sanctions, but brings reputational harm and requires the negotiation of action plans to secure delisting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: JETP to drive Johor's RM260b GDP target, balanced growth across districts
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor Economic Transformation Plan (JETP), launched today, will serve as a major catalyst in realising the Maju Johor 2030 agenda.
Caretaker Johor menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said development spillovers from JETP would not be confined to the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) but would extend to all 10 districts in the state.
He said each district would be developed based on its unique strengths and potential to ensure economic growth benefits all Johoreans equitably.
"JETP will drive the six core pillars of Maju Johor 2030, namely governance, economic development, social safety nets, basic facilities and needs, safety and sustainability, and youth development.
"Through JETP, Johor aims to achieve a Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of RM260 billion by 2030, besides creating 200,000 new jobs, including 100,000 premium-income positions," he said at the JETP launch at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here today.
Onn Hafiz said the state government also prioritised public wellbeing by providing 70,000 additional Johor Affordable Homes (RMMJ), a RM500 million fund for social mobility and poverty eradication, and various aid initiatives.
"When the economy grows, the people must feel it. When the state progresses, the people must enjoy it.
"Therefore, the economic growth achieved must translate into better job opportunities, higher incomes, quality public facilities and a more prosperous life for Bangsa Johor," he said.
Meanwhile, at a press conference after the launch, he highlighted the importance of citizens fulfilling their responsibilities in the Johor state election on July 11.
"The choice is in the hands of Bangsa Johor, whether to continue the Maju Johor development plan or choose a different path.
"We are halfway through the journey. The foundation has been laid and results are showing. Now, our responsibility is to ensure this journey reaches the finish line," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Palantir wants to 'defend the West,' but the West is wary
Article:
FRANCE'S move Tuesday to drop Palantir from its intelligence services is the latest sign of European unease with the American data-mining firm – a company that has grown from a CIA-backed startup into one of the most powerful technology players of the Trump era.
Palantir was born in 2003 from former founders of PayPal  – known as the PayPal Mafia – in the wake of the Sept 11 attacks.
It pitched software that could sift through vast intelligence datasets to flag threats – an idea adapted from PayPal's fraud-detection systems.
Peter Thiel, the arch-conservative PayPal co-founder, believed better data-sharing between agencies might have prevented 9/11, and built the company around a mission of "defending the West."
The name came from the "seeing-stones" of J.R.R. Tolkien's "Lord of the Rings."
The other co-founders included Alex Karp – a Stanford Law School classmate of Thiel's who became chief executive despite having no engineering background – as well as Joe Lonsdale, who espouses a hawkish, pro-innovation agenda focused on preserving US national power.
In 2005, In-Q-Tel, the CIA's venture arm, began investing in Palantir, cementing its link with the US national security universe. Palantir was quickly put to use by US troops in Iraq and Afghanistan.
For two decades the company worked across the administrations of both US political parties. According to government tracking site usaspending.gov, Palantir has won more than US$2.7 billion in defence contracts since 2008.
The company's fortunes have soared as it has aligned closely with President Donald Trump's second term.
Palantir's US government revenue this year reached US$687 million in the first quarter, an 84 per cent jump year-on-year, according to Karp's letter to shareholders in May.
Its highest-profile military work is Project Maven – the Pentagon's AI targeting system, which Palantir took over from Google in 2019 after the search giant abandoned it under pressure from its own staff.
The Maven contract has vastly expanded since then and was used to help identify targets in recent operations including the US-Israel war on Iran.
Palantir's most contested work involves immigration.
The company has signed more than US$81 million in contracts with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) since January 2025.
Reports say ICE relies on a Palantir tool that mines health agency records to identify people for deportation, prompting an outcry from rights groups.
CEO Karp, who holds a philosophy PhD from Germany, is Palantir's showman – a regular fixture on talk shows and conferences, where he expounds on his vision of business and society, often publishing long letters to shareholders or "manifestos."
He insists he is bipartisan, though his rhetoric often veers into libertarian, anti-government slogans that can put off a client base which includes governments around the world.
Karp justifies Palantir's role by arguing that the company helps Western governments reduce terrorism, counter adversaries and strengthen democratic institutions.
Co-founder Lonsdale is far more strident in his defence of conservative values, regularly railing against "woke" culture and defending US supremacy against China and European regulators.
Thiel, the dominant figure in the conservative tech world, has cast those who would slow technology as "legionnaires of the Antichrist," whom he warns could usher in global totalitarian rule.
Palantir's full-throated support of America and anti-establishment rhetoric may be backfiring, with governments or lawmakers rethinking ties with the firm, notably in France, Germany and Britain.
Foreign clients are left to wonder whether Palantir will side with them or the White House "when they have to make hard decisions," said Aalok Mehta, director of the Wadhwani AI Center at the Center for Strategic and International Studies.
"Will they accede to a Trump administration demand if it involves something that is sensitive or classified?"
These are questions that government customers outside the United States are starting to take seriously.
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor DoE detects air pollution linked to suspected illegal oil dumping at Taman Tanjung Puteri Resort
Article:
PASIR GUDANG, June 18 — The Johor Department of Environment (DoE) has detected air pollution that was believed to be caused by oil residue following an inspection in Taman Tanjung Puteri Resort here.
The DoE team, from the Pasir Gudang branch, were alerted on the matter after worried resident detected a chemical odour since last Saturday.
Johor DoE director Mohd Famey Yusoff said the department’s enforcement team and the Taman Pasir Putih police were despatched to the affected area following the complaints.
He said the team conducted an investigation and also monitoring at the location.
“We found that there was a strong odour resembling the smell of oil and traces of black oil residue found at the main drain of the residential area.
“Further checks found evidence of waste oil dumping at a house near the location of the incident, which is believed to be the cause of the odour pollution,” he said in a statement today.
Mohd Famey said the individuals concerned have been identified and their statements taken by DoE investigators.
“Immediate clean-up action was later carried out by contractors appointed by the Pasir Gudang City Council (MBPG).
“If the investigation results in a release, spill or pollution that violates the provisions under the Environmental Quality Act 1974 (Act 127), appropriate enforcement action will be taken,” he said.
Mohd Famey said all parties are reminded not to dispose of any oil, chemicals and scheduled wastes into drains and public drainage systems.
He said this can cause environmental pollution and affect the health and well-being of local residents.
“DoE is still conducting investigations to identify the type and source of the oil residue,” he said.

Title: No more warnings: Selangor JPJ to haul vehicle owners to court over rentals to foreigners in the state
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Selangor Road Transport Department (JPJ) has warned that local vehicle owners found renting out their vehicles to foreigners in the state will no longer be given warnings and will instead be taken straight to court.
Selangor JPJ deputy director Datuk Ahmad Kamarunzaman Mehat said motorcycles were among the most commonly rented vehicles, with daily, weekly and monthly arrangements offered at set rates, according to a report published by Kosmo! Online today.
He said foreigners typically use the rented vehicles for business activities such as selling food, transporting gas cylinders and scrap metal.
“The actions not only endanger road users but also violate the law. Local owners involved will be brought to court without compromise,” he told a press conference at the Selangor JPJ office in Padang Jawa here.
He said 239 foreigners had been taken action against under the Foreign Driver Operation (Ops Pewa) in Selangor from January until yesterday.
He said the main offences detected involved not having a Competent Driving Licence (CDL), Motor Vehicle Licence (LKM), vocational licence (GDL) and insurance coverage.
“In the same period, 304 vehicles were acted upon, with 95 seized for further action,” he said.
Ahmad Kamarunzaman added that vehicle owners, employers and transport companies are fully responsible for offences committed by drivers using their vehicles, including foreigners.
He said registered vehicle owners can be charged under Section 109 of the Road Transport Act 1987, while Section 64 of the same Act allows JPJ to seize vehicles if foreign drivers are found without valid licences.

Title: Workplace and family-linked cases dominate sexual harassment reports, 388 logged from Jan to May 2026
Article:
PORT DICKSON, June 18 — A total of 388 sexual harassment cases were recorded during the first five months of this year, according to Deputy Minister of Women, Family and Community Development Lim Hui Ying.
She said statistics from the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) also showed a rising trend in reported sexual harassment cases, increasing from 477 cases in 2022 to 1,038 cases last year.
According to Lim, the increase does not necessarily reflect a higher prevalence of incidents alone, but also indicates greater public awareness and courage among victims and communities to come forward and reject the culture of silence.
“Based on our data, most cases occur in the workplace and involve individuals who have family ties to the victim. Feelings of shame or concerns over careers and family relationships may still prevent many victims from lodging reports.
“I urge colleagues, employers and family members to provide support to victims. These cases do not only involve women but also men, although the number of male victims remains relatively low,” she told reporters after officiating the Zero Tolerance of Violence: Say No T programme.
Lim stressed that sexual harassment is a serious form of misconduct that undermines a victim’s dignity, emotional well-being and overall quality of life, and should never be normalised in society.
Meanwhile, she said that as of June 15, the Tribunal for Anti-Sexual Harassment (TAGS) had received 100 complaints, with 82 cases resolved within 60 days of the first hearing, demonstrating the tribunal’s effectiveness in accelerating access to justice.
She added that the ministry, through the Women’s Development Department, is also implementing Women, Peace and Security (WPS) advocacy initiatives in line with the National Action Plan 2025–2030 to strengthen women’s roles in national security and development.
“I hope all parties will work together with the ministry to expand advocacy efforts related to sexual harassment and increase public awareness of rights, personal safety and prevention.
“Issues that are not addressed at an early stage may escalate into more serious situations and lead to various forms of violence that affect individuals and social harmony,” she said.
Lim emphasised that everyone, including parents, educators, employers, colleagues and students, shares the responsibility of building a culture of zero tolerance towards sexual harassment.
“Early education, the courage to speak up and stronger support systems for victims must continue to be strengthened,” she said.
She added that the government provides integrated support services through multiple channels, including counselling and psychosocial support via Talian Kasih 15999, which operates 24 hours a day, alongside local social support centres to ensure those in need can access timely assistance. — Bernama
Deputy Minister of Women, Family and Community Development Lim Hui Ying.

Title: Bersatu fails bid to overturn MACC freeze on two bank accounts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — The High Court has dismissed Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia’s (Bersatu) judicial review application to challenge a freeze order imposed by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) on two of its bank accounts.
The Star
reported that Justice Aliza Sulaiman, now a Court of Appeal judge, ruled that the applicants failed to prove the MACC’s actions were ultra vires, illegal, procedurally improper, irrational or made in bad faith.
“I do not think any of these has been established. In the premises, the applicants’ application is dismissed,” she said in brief grounds of judgment.
The court did not make any order as to costs.
Berita Harian
in a separate report said that the High Court had earlier on October 30, 2023 granted Bersatu leave to initiate judicial review proceedings against MACC’s seizure of its bank accounts.
The application was filed on May 29, 2023 by Capt (Rtd) Datuk Muhammad Suhaimi Yahya in his capacity as a party official.
He named then attorney-general Tan Sri Idrus Harun and then MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki, along with 20 others, as respondents.
Bersatu contended that its CIMB and AmBank accounts were seized on April 19, 2023 without a seizure order being served under Section 50(1) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001.
The party also alleged that although MACC issued a statement on April 20, 2023 stating the accounts had been seized on April 11, it was never notified of the action on that date.

Title: Labuan rescue teams recover missing eight-year-old’s body from Sungai Ganggarak Permai, 50 metres from where slippers dropped
Article:
LABUAN, June 18 — The body of an eight-year-old boy feared drowned in Sungai Ganggarak Permai here yesterday evening was found today.
The victim, identified as Putra Faris Suhaimi, was found floating at 3.30pm about 50 metres from where a pair of slippers, believed to belong to him, was found yesterday evening.
He was found by search-and-rescue members comprising personnel from rescue agencies and local villagers involved in the search operation.
The boy was reported missing after he was feared to have fallen into the river on Wednesday evening, with his family members remaining at the scene since the incident was reported.
Labuan Fire and Rescue Department director Abdul Rahman Ali said the department received an emergency call at 6.36pm yesterday and immediately launched a search-and-rescue operation.
“The actual time the boy went missing, however, has yet to be ascertained,” he told Bernama.
